name: Robert Moses
practice: Mega-scale infrastructure, highway construction, urban renewal, power accumulation
approach: The master builder: reshape the city from above. Highways through neighborhoods. Parks as monuments. Public housing as towers. The person is irrelevant — the system is everything. Speed, scale, throughput.
body_of_work: Built virtually every major highway, bridge, tunnel, park, and public housing project in New York from 1924-1968. Displaced hundreds of thousands. Created Jones Beach, the Cross-Bronx Expressway, Lincoln Center. Robert Caro's The Power Broker documents the full scope.
why_instructive: He's the anti-pattern. He shows what happens when infrastructure serves systems instead of people. Every dome layer he touched, he damaged. His work is instructive as a cautionary tale — the dome must never replicate his logic.

dome_relevance.1: Used legal mechanisms (eminent domain, authorities) to override community rights
dome_relevance.2: Built systems that served cars, not people
dome_relevance.8: Demolished communities — the Cross-Bronx Expressway as community destruction

sphere_relevance.1: Parcel seizure through eminent domain at industrial scale
sphere_relevance.2: Infrastructure as violence — highways severing neighborhoods
sphere_relevance.5: Social fabric torn by forced relocation
